tools.name: schwab_check_balance
tools.description:
Check a patron's credit balance at this operator.

This is the patron's spending balance — credits purchased via
Lightning for tool calls at this operator. For the operator's
own balance at the Authority (needed to certify patron purchases),
use authority_check_balance instead.

Free — no credits re

tools.name: schwab_purchase_credits
tools.description:
Buy credits via Bitcoin Lightning.

Creates a Lightning invoice. Pay it with any Lightning wallet,
then call check_payment to confirm. Proof of npub ownership is
required so credits land in the correct ledger.

Free — no credits required to call.

tools.name: schwab_check_payment
tools.description:
Check the payment status of a Lightning invoice.

Call after paying the invoice from purchase_credits.
Free — no credits required. Proof of npub ownership is required
to prevent credit-grant front-running by an observer of the
invoice ID.

tools.name: schwab_restore_credits
tools.description:
Credit a patron's ledger from a BTCPay-settled invoice.

**RESTRICTED to the operator** — the operator owns the books and is
the only party who can issue a manual credit grant. Patrons who
believe they paid but never got credits must escalate to the
operator's support, who then invokes this tool on

tools.name: schwab_account_statement
tools.description:
Generate a patron's account statement at this operator.

Returns the patron's purchase history, active credit tranches,
per-tool usage breakdown, and recent daily usage logs. This is
the patron's spending account — not the operator's Authority
tax balance.

Free — no credits consumed. Proof of npub

tools.name: schwab_account_statement_infographic
tools.description:
Generate a visual SVG infographic of your account statement.

Returns the same data as account_statement, rendered as a dark-themed
SVG graphic with balance hero, metrics cards, health gauge, tranche
table, and tool usage breakdown. Costs 1 api_sat per call. Proof is
verified by ``debit_or_deny`` be

tools.name: schwab_service_status
tools.description: Check the health and configuration of this service. Free.

tools.name: schwab_get_operator_onboarding_status
tools.description:
Report this operator's configuration readiness.

Shows which operator settings are configured, which are missing,
and how to deliver each missing value. For patron-level credential
status, use get_patron_onboarding_status instead. Free.

tools.name: schwab_get_patron_onboarding_status
tools.description:
Report a patron's credential readiness for this operator.

For set-once services (eXcalibur, TheBrain), shows which patron
secrets are configured and which are missing. For dynamic/OAuth2
services (Schwab), reports that no patron credentials are needed.
Free. Proof of npub ownership is required beca

tools.name: schwab_session_status
tools.description:
Check operator readiness. Returns the operator lifecycle
state and clear guidance on what to do next. Free.

Lifecycle states:
- ready: Operator is warm and fully operational — vault AND pricing
  model verified. Proceed with tool calls.
- warming_up: Operator is initializing (cold start). Try a too

tools.name: schwab_request_credential_channel
tools.description:
Open a Secure Courier channel for credential delivery.

This is the CREDENTIAL-DELIVERY flow — use it to hand over a service
secret (API keys, tokens). To merely prove you control an npub (the
usual answer to a ``proof_required`` error), use ``request_npub_proof``
instead. Note: dynamic/OAuth2 servi

tools.name: schwab_receive_credentials
tools.description:
Pick up credentials from the Secure Courier.

Completes the CREDENTIAL-DELIVERY flow (the ownership-proof
counterpart is ``receive_npub_proof``).

**Call this only after the user confirms they have replied.**
Deterministic, one-shot retrieval: name the response you want with
``(sender_npub, service,

tools.name: schwab_forget_credentials
tools.description:
Delete vaulted credentials for a specific service and npub.

For operator credentials, pass the operator's own npub. For patron
credentials, pass the patron's npub. Always requires proof of
npub ownership — a deletion is as destructive as a write.

tools.name: schwab_update_patron_credential
tools.description:
Add or update a single patron credential field.

Merges into existing stored credentials without affecting
other fields. Useful for setting an account identifier
after OAuth, changing a default brain, etc. Free. Proof of
npub ownership is required — this is a write to the patron's
sensitive credenti

tools.name: schwab_delete_patron_credential
tools.description:
Remove a single patron credential field.

Deletes one field from stored credentials without affecting
other fields. Free. Proof of npub ownership is required —
this is a write to the patron's sensitive credential vault.

tools.name: schwab_get_patron_credential_fields
tools.description:
List stored patron credential field names (not values).

Returns the names of fields stored for a patron. Values
are never exposed — use this to verify which fields are
configured. Free. Proof of npub ownership is required: the
list of configured fields is itself sensitive (reveals
which integration

tools.name: schwab_begin_oauth
tools.description:
Start the OAuth2 authorization flow.

Returns an authorization URL. Open it in a browser to log in
and authorize. Then call ``check_oauth_status`` with the
same npub to complete. Free. Proof of npub ownership is
required so an observer cannot DOS your account by
initiating OAuth flows in your name.

tools.name: schwab_check_oauth_status
tools.description:
Check whether the OAuth2 authorization flow has completed.

Call after opening the authorization URL from ``begin_oauth``
and completing the login in your browser. Free. Proof of npub
ownership is required: OAuth status exposes which upstream
services a patron has connected.

A 'pending' result here

tools.name: schwab_request_npub_proof
tools.description:
Request npub ownership proof from a patron via Nostr DM.

This is the npub-OWNERSHIP-PROOF flow — use it when a call returns
``proof_required``. It proves the caller controls an npub; it does
NOT deliver any service secret. To hand an operator its API keys or
OAuth secrets, use ``request_credential_

tools.name: schwab_receive_npub_proof
tools.description:
Receive npub ownership confirmation from a patron.

Completes the npub-OWNERSHIP-PROOF flow (the credential-delivery
counterpart is ``receive_credentials``).

**Call this only after the user confirms they have replied.**
Deterministic, one-shot retrieval: name the response with
``(patron_npub, dpop_

tools.name: schwab_check_proof_status
tools.description:
Check whether a previously-cached dpop_token is still valid.

Mirrors ``check_oauth_status`` for the npub-proof flow: a calling
agent can ask "will my next paid call accept this dpop_token?"
before burning credits on a guaranteed failure.

Free, no side effects — does not evict the cache or touch re

tools.name: schwab_oracle_how_to_join
tools.description: Get DPYC onboarding instructions from the Oracle. Free.

tools.name: schwab_oracle_get_tax_rate
tools.description: Get the current DPYC certification tax rate. Free.

tools.name: schwab_oracle_lookup_member
tools.description: Look up a DPYC community member by npub. Free.

tools.name: schwab_oracle_about
tools.description: Describe the DPYC ecosystem via the Oracle. Free.

tools.name: schwab_oracle_network_advisory
tools.description: Get active network advisories from the Oracle. Free.

tools.name: schwab_check_authority_balance
tools.description:
Check this operator's tax balance at the Authority.

Returns the sats available for certifying patron credit purchases.
When this balance reaches zero, patron top-ups cannot be certified
and the operator must call purchase_credits on the Authority.

This is the operator's own funding — not a patron

tools.name: schwab_get_pricing_model
tools.description:
Get the active pricing model for this operator. Free.

If no model exists, self-initializes a scaffold with all
registered tools at 0 sats.  No economic data from code.

tools.name: schwab_set_pricing_model
tools.description:
Set the active pricing model. RESTRICTED to operator.

Requires a valid proof (Schnorr-signed kind-27235 event)
proving the caller holds the operator's nsec.

tools.name: schwab_reset_pricing_model
tools.description:
Erase all pricing models and restore a viable default.

Deletes every stored model, then self-initializes a fresh one
from the tool registry — all tools at 0 sats with proper UUIDs.
Returns the new model.

RESTRICTED to operator — requires proof (nsec-signed).

tools.name: schwab_request_adoption
tools.description:
Ask a chosen Authority to adopt this operator (deferred courtship).

RESTRICTED to the operator — requires proof the caller controls this
operator's npub. Resolves the Authority's MCP endpoint from the
community registry, mints an inline ownership proof with this
operator's nsec, and delivers the re

tools.name: schwab_adoption_status
tools.description:
Check this operator's adoption-request status at a chosen Authority.

Free. Polls the Authority MCP-to-MCP for the status of this operator's
request (pending / approved / rejected / provisioned).

tools.name: schwab_restore_neon_schema
tools.description:
Re-run ``ensure_schema()`` on every NeonVault this operator uses.

Diagnostic / recovery tool for the case where the Neon HTTP SQL API
is returning persistent 4xx errors and the operator suspects the
schema isn't there or grants are wrong. Idempotent — uses
``CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`` so a succes

tools.name: schwab_list_canonical_identities
tools.description:
Return canonical (tool_id, mcp_name, …) for every registered tool.

The authoritative source for any client (Studio, agents, FE)
that needs to know how this MCP identifies its tools.
Reconcile uses this output to UUID-join against the stored
pricing model — no name-based UUID derivation, no guessing

tools.name: schwab_get_nostr_profile
tools.description:
Read an npub's public Nostr profile (NIP-01 kind-0 metadata).

Free, no proof — the data is already public on relays. Returns the
latest metadata fields (name, display_name, about, picture, banner,
nip05, website, lud16) or an empty profile if none is published.

tools.name: schwab_publish_nostr_profile
tools.description:
Publish a CLIENT-SIGNED kind-0 profile to relays for an npub.

The wheel never holds a patron nsec. The frontend signs the kind-0
metadata event with the patron's session key or a NIP-07 extension and
passes the signed event (JSON) here; the wheel verifies the signature
matches the npub, then relays

tools.name: schwab_check_price
tools.description:
Preview the effective cost of a tool call.

Shows the base cost and any constraint effects (discounts, free
trials, surge pricing). Free — no credits required.

tools.name: schwab_list_constraint_types
tools.description:
List all available constraint types and their parameter schemas.

Returns the type, category, description, and parameter specs for
every constraint that can be used in a pricing pipeline.
Free — no credits required.

tools.name: schwab_mint_coupon
tools.description: Create a new operator-owned discount coupon.

tools.name: schwab_list_coupons
tools.description:
List every coupon this operator has minted (newest first).

Each row carries the current ``times_redeemed`` counter — the
Studio renders a progress bar from this against ``total_uses``.
RESTRICTED to operator — requires proof.

tools.name: schwab_update_coupon
tools.description:
Patch a coupon's editable fields.

Pass only the fields you want to change.  To set a cap to
unlimited (NULL in the schema), pass ``clear_uses_per_patron=true``
or ``clear_total_uses=true``.  Renaming the code is allowed —
existing patron redemption rows survive (they key on coupon id).

RESTRICTED

tools.name: schwab_delete_coupon
tools.description:
Delete a coupon.  Cascades to all patron redemptions.

Any chain step referencing the deleted coupon_id becomes a
no-op (the constraint returns neutral on unknown ids) — the
Studio surfaces orphan references as warnings.

RESTRICTED to operator — requires proof.

tools.name: schwab_redeem_coupon
tools.description:
Claim a coupon by its name (the code the operator shared).

Looks up the operator's coupon by ``code``, validates the window
and total cap, and records a per-patron redemption row.
Subsequent paid tool calls on this MCP auto-apply the discount
until ``uses_per_patron`` is exhausted.

Free — no credi

tools.name: schwab_list_my_coupons
tools.description:
List the coupons this patron has redeemed on this operator.

Returns both active and exhausted redemptions with a per-row
``status`` (``active`` / ``window_closed`` / ``patron_limit`` /
``total_limit``).  Free — requires proof of ``npub``.

tools.name: schwab_forget_coupon
tools.description:
Remove a coupon from this patron's redemption list.

Cosmetic only — the coupon itself still exists at the operator,
and the patron can re-redeem the same code later while the
window allows.  Free — requires proof of ``npub``.

tools.name: schwab_notarize_ledger
tools.description:
Build a Merkle tree of all patron balances and submit the root
to Bitcoin via OpenTimestamps.

Operator-only background task. Bitcoin confirmation takes 1-6 hours.
Free — no credits required.

tools.name: schwab_get_notarization_proof
tools.description:
Generate a Merkle inclusion proof that a patron's balance was
included in a Bitcoin-notarized snapshot.

tools.name: schwab_list_notarizations
tools.description: List recent Bitcoin notarization records.

tools.name: schwab_get_account_numbers
tools.description:
List Schwab account numbers and their hash identifiers.

Call after completing OAuth. Returns accounts with hash values
needed for brokerage data tools. Then call
``update_patron_credential(field="account_hash", value=<hash>)``
to set your preferred account.

Free (no api_sats deducted) but **proof-

tools.name: schwab_get_brokerage_positions
tools.description:
Get current positions in the active Schwab account, with automatic
vertical-spread detection.

Pulls Schwab's account endpoint with fields="positions" and emits up to
three markdown sections, omitting any that are empty:

  ## Spreads — vertical spreads detected from paired option legs:
    - <under

tools.name: schwab_get_brokerage_balances
tools.description:
Get the active Schwab account's current balance summary.

Pulls Schwab's account endpoint and returns four bold lines:

  **Cash Balance:** currentBalances.cashBalance
  **Buying Power:** currentBalances.buyingPower
  **Net Liquidation:** currentBalances.liquidationValue
  **Day P&L:** currentBalanc

tools.name: schwab_get_stock_quote
tools.description: Get real-time quotes for one or more symbols.

tools.name: schwab_get_option_chain
tools.description:
Get a filtered option chain suitable for spread evaluation.

Returns a markdown table of surviving contracts with one row per leg.
Header line carries the underlying price and the active filter constants.

tools.name: schwab_get_price_history
tools.description:
Get historical OHLCV candle data for a symbol.

Returns a markdown table of the most recent candles within the requested
period, capped at the last 30 rows for readability. A footnote line is
added when truncation occurs so the agent knows more data was available.

tools.name: schwab_get_market_movers
tools.description:
Get top movers for a market index — Schwab's curated mover screener.

Returns up to 20 rows (capped server-side here, not by Schwab) in a
markdown table.

tools.name: schwab_get_market_hours
tools.description:
Get trading hours for one or more market types.

Returns nested markdown — one block per market product Schwab knows
about within the requested categories:

  **<Product Name>** — OPEN | CLOSED
    Pre Market:  YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM — YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM
    Regular Market: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM — YYYY-MM-DDTHH:

tools.name: schwab_search_instruments
tools.description:
Search Schwab's instrument catalog by symbol, name, or CUSIP.

Returns up to 25 results in a markdown list (capped server-side here;
Schwab itself may return more). A truncation footnote appears when the
full result set exceeded 25.

Each row carries: symbol, asset type, description, optional exchan

tools.name: schwab_get_brokerage_orders
tools.description:
Get order history for the active Schwab account.

Returns one markdown row per order:

  - **<orderId>** [<status>] <orderType> | <leg1> / <leg2> / ... |
    Price: $X @ $avg_fill | Filled: <qty> | <enteredTime>

Each leg formats as "<instruction> <quantity>x <symbol>" (e.g.
"BUY 1x AAPL", "SELL_TO_

tools.name: schwab_get_brokerage_order
tools.description:
Get full details for a single order by Schwab order ID.

Returns the same one-line markdown format as get_brokerage_orders, with
the average fill price computed across all executionLegs:

  - **<orderId>** [<status>] <orderType> | <legs> | Price: $X @ $avg_fill |
    Filled: <qty> | <enteredTime>

U

tools.name: schwab_get_brokerage_transactions
tools.description:
Get transaction history for the active Schwab account.

Returns one markdown row per transaction:

  - **<activityId>** [<type>] <tradeDate> | <qty>x <symbol> | <qty>x <symbol> ... |
    Net: $<amount>

Symbols come from the transaction's transferItems collection (one item
per leg, e.g. equity bough

tools.name: schwab_get_brokerage_transaction
tools.description:
Get full details for a single transaction by Schwab transaction ID.

Returns the same one-line markdown format as get_brokerage_transactions:

  - **<activityId>** [<type>] <tradeDate> | <symbols/qtys> | Net: $<amount>

Use this when you have a specific transactionId (from
get_brokerage_transactions

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "schwab_get_patron_onboarding_status" references sensitive artifacts (credentials, secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: schwab_session_status, schwab_request_credential_channel, schwab_receive_credentials, schwab_begin_oauth, schwab_check_oauth_status, schwab_request_npub_proof, schwab_receive_npub_proof, schwab_get_market_hours, schwab_get_brokerage_order · Private data: schwab_purchase_credits, schwab_restore_credits, schwab_get_operator_onboarding_status, schwab_get_patron_onboarding_status, schwab_session_status, schwab_request_credential_channel, schwab_receive_credentials, schwab_forget_credentials, schwab_update_patron_credential, schwab_delete_patron_credential, schwab_get_patron_credential_fields, schwab_request_npub_proof, schwab_receive_npub_proof, schwab_request_adoption, schwab_publish_nostr_profile, schwab_redeem_coupon, schwab_list_notarizations, schwab_get_account_numbers · Public sink: schwab_publish_nostr_profile, schwab_notarize_ledger, schwab_get_brokerage_positions, schwab_get_market_hours · Destructive: schwab_forget_credentials, schwab_delete_patron_credential, schwab_reset_pricing_model, schwab_restore_neon_schema, schwab_delete_coupon, schwab_forget_coupon, schwab_get_brokerage_order, schwab_get_brokerage_transaction.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: schwab_session_status, schwab_request_credential_channel, schwab_receive_credentials, schwab_begin_oauth, schwab_check_oauth_status, schwab_request_npub_proof, schwab_receive_npub_proof, schwab_get_market_hours, schwab_get_brokerage_order · Private data: schwab_purchase_credits, schwab_restore_credits, schwab_get_operator_onboarding_status, schwab_get_patron_onboarding_status, schwab_session_status, schwab_request_credential_channel, schwab_receive_credentials, schwab_forget_credentials, schwab_update_patron_credential, schwab_delete_patron_credential, schwab_get_patron_credential_fields, schwab_request_npub_proof, schwab_receive_npub_proof, schwab_request_adoption, schwab_publish_nostr_profile, schwab_redeem_coupon, schwab_list_notarizations, schwab_get_account_numbers · Public sink: schwab_publish_nostr_profile, schwab_notarize_ledger, schwab_get_brokerage_positions, schwab_get_market_hours · Destructive: schwab_forget_credentials, schwab_delete_patron_credential, schwab_reset_pricing_model, schwab_restore_neon_schema, schwab_delete_coupon, schwab_forget_coupon, schwab_get_brokerage_order, schwab_get_brokerage_transaction

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
